content.claim: In OECD countries, needs-based allocation of education entitlements (typical in most systems) differs structurally from diagnosis-gated allocation (as in Israel), with diagnosis-gated systems theorized to create stronger incentives toward identification but this distinction is not directly tested across systems
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Theoretical causal link between diagnosis-gating and overidentification not empirically demonstrated in source
Therapy hours standardization in Israel is diagnosis-linked but outcomes data not provided
Multi-disciplinary assessment standard noted but its actual implementation rigor across countries not assessed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
